title: Fluffy Romantic Saudi Arabian Rice Pilaf
description: A cultured and fluffy Saudi Arabian dish, this romantic rice pilaf is made with warm water and features a mortar-pestle-pound method, making it jain-friendly and perfect for a special occasion.
instructions: Heat the ghee or oil in a claypot over medium heat.
Add the cumin seeds and coriander seeds and let them sizzle for a few seconds.
Add the chopped onion and saute until it is translucent.
Add the minced garlic and saute for another minute.
Add the rinsed and drained basmati rice and stir to coat the rice with the ghee or oil.
Add the warm water, salt, black pepper, and cardamom powder to the claypot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and cover the claypot with a tight-fitting lid.
Simmer for 15-20 minutes or until the water is absorbed and the rice is fluffy.
Use a mortar and pestle to pound the cooked rice gently to separate the grains.
Serve the fluffy romantic Saudi Arabian rice pilaf hot.
